description: React Server Components (RSC) Flight protocol attacks. Covers CVE-2025-55182 React2Shell (Wiz) and CVE-2025-66478 (Praetorian) — Next.js boundary-deserialization RCE via crafted x-rsc-form-state / Server Action bodies. Affects ~39% of cloud Next.js apps (App Router 13.x-15.x with Server Actions enabled). Markers: text/x-component, Next-Action header, $1@/$2@ RSC chunk syntax.

rsc_flight_fingerprint.description: Passive detection of RSC Flight responses. Confirms target uses Server Components — prerequisite for the React2Shell / boundary-deserialization chain.

server_action_invocation_anomaly.description: Server Actions invoked via Next-Action header. Probe checks whether arbitrary action IDs accept attacker-controlled args via x-www-form-urlencoded body or multipart FormData (CVE-2025-66478 boundary).

probes.description: Detect Next-Action handshake — server echoes action ID in response

RSC Flight body with bogus serialized child — CVE-2025-55182 React2Shell parser entry. 500 with React parser error or 200 with attacker payload reflected indicates parser executes attacker-supplied Flight chunks.

rsc_form_state_pollution.description: x-rsc-form-state form-state-restoration channel — accepts pickled form state from client. CVE-2025-66478 abuses this for boundary deserialization. Probe submits crafted state to verify server-side replay.

probes.description: Prototype-polluting form-state replay — server unpickles into Object.prototype

rsc_action_id_enumeration.description: Server Action IDs are content-hashed but enumerable from JS bundles. Probe greps build artefacts for action IDs and confirms unauthenticated invocation.

probes.description: Build chunk listing — leaks action IDs that should require auth
